auth.redaction: the bearer token must never appear in exception text, repr, or logs

submit.body: run_name, tasks (array of task specs passed through verbatim: task_key plus notebook_task/spark_python_task/depends_on/...), idempotency_token, optional timeout_seconds

shape.run_id: int64 — canonical id of the new one-time run

get_run.success: 200 with the Run object: run_id, run_page_url, status {state, termination_details}, legacy state {life_cycle_state, result_state, state_message} (deprecated in 2.2 but still served), tasks [{run_id, task_key, status, state, attempt_number}], next_page_token when >100 tasks

get_output.note: output is per task run. A multi-task parent run has no output of its own — the endpoint returns 400 INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE for the parent run_id; call it once per task run_id from the run's tasks array. Only the first 5 MiB of output are returned.
get_output.success: 200 with RunOutput: notebook_output {result, truncated} | sql_output | error, error_trace, logs, logs_truncated, metadata

cancel.note: cancellation is asynchronous — the run may still be running when the call returns; keep polling get_run until a terminal state

idempotency.semantics: if a run already exists for the token, submit does not create a new run and returns the existing run_id; after a transport or 5xx failure the same token must be resent so exactly one run is ever launched

rate_limit.policy: sleep exactly the Retry-After seconds through the injected sleep, retry, and give up with a typed error after max_retries retries

transient_retry.policy: submit may be retried only because idempotency_token makes it safe; the same token must be reused

test_fixture_notes.task_run_ids: parent run_id * 10 + task position (fixture convention only)
